data.title: Signature Global acquires 8.39-acre land along Dwarka Expressway in Gurugram; eyes Rs 3,200-crore revenue
data.description:
Signature Global acquires 8.38-acre land at along Dwarka Expressway in Gurugram; eyes Rs 3,200 crore revenue

Delhi NCR-based listed real estate developer, Signature Global (India) Limited has acquired 8.39 acres of land along Dwarka Expressway for Rs 282.42 crore, the company said.

The sale deed of the land, which is located at Sector 37D in Gurugram, was executed on March 20. The overall developable potential of the acquired land would be approximately 2 million square feet, the company said in statement on March 21.

At 11:40 am, Signature Global (India) was quoting at Rs 1,151.85, up Rs 37.30, or 3.35 percent, on the BSE on March 21.

The company intends to launch a luxury housing project on this land parcel by the end of next financial year (2025-26) and it expects total revenue of around Rs 3,200 crore.

Pradeep Aggarwal, Founder and Chairman, Signature Global, said that Gurugram’s housing market has experienced exceptional growth in recent years, with Dwarka Expressway emerging as India’s fastest-growing real estate corridor.

“Strategically located between Delhi and Gurugram, Dwarka Expressway offers unparalleled access to key business hubs, the airport, and an expanding metro network. Among the standout locations, sector 37D has rapidly become a key investment hotspot. Given the success of our past projects, we are confident that our upcoming development in this sector will receive an overwhelming response upon launch,” he said.

According to a CREDAI-Colliers-Liases Foras report, housing prices here have surged by a record 58 percent YoY along this corridor. Seamless connectivity, robust infrastructure, and strong investment potential are some of the driving factors behind such a price growth along Dwarka Expressway.

Signature Global owns 45.1-acre land bank in Gurugram's Sector 37D, also known as DXP Estate, wherein the company is developing both multi-story and low-rise residential units.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

In the first nine months of FY 2024-25, the company added approximately 2.9 million square feet of saleable area to this location. In September 2024, Signature Global had awarded a Rs 1,144 crore construction contract to Ahluwalia Contracts (India) Ltd. for its flagship residential project “De-luxe DXP” located along the Dwarka Expressway.

The company has so far delivered 13.5 million sq. ft. of housing area and has a robust pipeline of approximately 46 million sq. ft. in its forthcoming and ongoing projects, set for execution over the next 2-3 years.

In Q3FY25 the company achieved pre-sales of about Rs 2,770 crore and recognised Rs 830 crore revenue.
data.author: MoneyControl

data.title: Sebi took down over 70,000 misleading handles of unregistered advisors in six months
data.description:
Since October 2024, SEBI has worked to take down over 70,000 misleading handles: SEBIs Ananth Narayan

The capital market regulator has intensified its crackdown on social media handles misleading investors, taking down more than 70,000 such accounts during the past six months, at a time when domestic ownership of equities has been steadily rising.

With domestic mutual fund inflow into equities has been near record levels, Sebi said curbing fraudulent activities by unregistered advisors becomes a high priority. Whole-time Member Ananth Narayan said on March 21, during his address at the ARIA (Association of Registered Investment Advisors) Aspire 2025 event.

"Since October 2024, SEBI has worked with social media companies to take down over 70,000 misleading handles," he said, proposing investors use the UPI pay-right handle to identify registered entities, and ensure investors make payments only to Sebi-registered entities. "If we can create this safe space, it will do so much to allow us to filter out the riffraff, and ensure that we do all the good things required for this gated safe community," Ananth Narayan added.

"…we need to ensure investor protection, awareness, and responsible investing," Narayan said, adding that "sustained capital formation is our collective goal." Narayan reiterated the importance of collaboration between all market participants (issuers, investors, regulators, and advisors) to maintain the momentum of capital formation and trust in the financial markets.

FPI and Domestic Fund Flow

Since April 2020, the total FPI inflow into India amounts to a staggering $54 billion, with equity flow contributing $21 billion and debt inflow adding $33 billion. This contrasts significantly with the previous five-year period, between FY16-20, when inflows totalled only $19 billion, with equity flow accounting for $12 billion and debt flows 7 billion, Narayan recalled.

Ananth Narayan said that foreign investors remain heavily committed to the Indian market, given that the value of FPI holding in India as of February 2025 amounted to over Rs 62 lakh crore in equity, and Rs 5.9 lakh crores in debt.

"The recent trend of higher FPI debt flows following India's inclusion in global debt indices has improved the portfolio mix," Narayan explained. "For a growing country like ours, that is not a bad outcome."

On the balance between equity and debt investment, Narayan said that India’s nominal GDP growth rate of 10-12 percent per year offers an opportunity for debt financing at a rate of 7-8 percent.

Narayan said India must continue to deliver on sustained economic growth, stable macroeconomic policies, and sound governance to maintain investor confidence. "This is not to suggest complacency anywhere," he said. "We need foreign savings, and we remain committed to drawing in foreign investments to fund capital formation in India."

While FPI play a crucial role, Narayan said the importance of domestic investment has been on the rise. Domestic mutual fund inflow into equity markets has reached unprecedented levels. "Over the past five fiscal years, domestic mutual funds have seen inflows of over Rs 16 lakh crore into equity schemes," he said, pointing out that this is ten times the net inflow from FPIs into equity during the same period.

The rising domestic mutual fund participation has significantly altered the composition of India’s market capitalisation. As of December 2024, mutual funds accounted for 10 percent of NSE’s total market capitalisation, up from just 3.9 percent in December 2014. On the other hand, FPI’s share of market capitalisation fell from 22 percent to 17.4 percent during the same period.

"This trend of Indians owning more equity is not necessarily bad," Narayan said, adding that it places the onus on the ecosystem to ensure we realise our growth aspirations and potential in sustained capital formation.

He also stressed on the importance of good governance and responsible financial planning. "Issuers must deliver on growth and governance. Investors are putting a lot of trust and faith in advisors," he said.

Narayan added that going forward, he sees a need to further leverage the accredited investor model, to identify risk-aware and risk-capable investors, so they can more freely fund risk-takers, in a light-touch regulatory environment. “If we can identify investors who don't need our protection, I think we would be very happy to make that entire experience as light-touch as possible,” he said, adding that the dialogue between Sebi and the intersections of investment advisors, non-discretionary PMS and mutual fund distributors should continue.

Narayan said that spreading investor awareness and education is a core part of Sebi’s remit, and the regulator is working to enhance investor outreach. "We are also undertaking a nationwide survey to help inform our outreach strategy," he added.
data.author: MoneyControl

data.title: Nifty, Sensex extend gains for fifth session led by auto, financial stocks; IT names pare losses
data.description:
The Nifty and Sensex started the session on a weak footing but soon managed to shrug off jitters.

Buying made its way into the market as benchmark indices--the Nifty 50 and Sensex, extended the uptrend to the fifth consecutive session as of mid-trade on March 21. Strong gains in automobile and financial names drove the upmove, offsetting losses in information technology heavyweights like Infosys.

With the uptick in trade, the Nifty 50 also managed to move past the 23,300-mark, a move that market experts were largely anticipating. The turnaround for Indian equities seen in the past week was also triggered by changing sentiment among foreign institutional investors turned net buyers in two out of the past three sessions, after a month of relentless selling.

At 11.25 am, the Sensex was up 512.58 points or 0.67 percent at 76,860.64, and the Nifty was up 150.50 points or 0.65 percent at 23,341.15. The market breadth favoured gainers as about 2,599 shares rose, 808 fell, and 115 were unchanged.

Follow our market blog to catch all the live action

Technical factors also supported expectations of a sustained bullish momentum for Indian equities as the Nifty 50 managed to break through its 50-day exponential moving average (EMA) in the previous session. With that done,  Sameet Chavan, Head Research, Technical and Derivative at Angel One,  is eyeing the 89 EMA around 23,300 - 23,400, making it an immediate hurdle, followed by the previous high at 23,800 as the next key resistance.

On the flip side, Chavan sees immediate support is seen at 23,000, aligning with the 50-day EMA, while strong support lies at 22,800.

On the sectoral front, 11 out of the 13 major sectoral indices were trading in the green, with Nifty Realty being the frontrunner, up over 2 percent.

(This is a developing story, please come back for more.)
data.author: MoneyControl

data.title: Sensex jumps 550 pts, Nifty above 23,350: Easing foreign selling among key factors behind market gain for 5th day
data.description:
Sensex jumps 500 pts, Nifty above 23,300: Easing foreign selling among key factors behind market gain for 5th day

Benchmark indices Sensex and Nifty rose for fifth straight day on March 21 as stronger rupee and recovery in IT shares led to the rally. At 11:34 am, the Sensex was up 592.97 points or 0.78 percent at 76,941.03, and the Nifty was up 175.80 points or 0.76 percent at 23,366.45. About 2593 shares advanced, 836 shares declined, and 112 shares unchanged.

Here are the key factors behind the market rally:

1) Stronger rupee: Indian rupee opened 14 paise up on March 21 due to support from strong foreign investors flows and fall in real effective exchange rate (REER), currency experts said.

The local currency opened at 86.2287 against the US dollar, and then traded at 86.2337 against the US dollar, as compared to previous close of 86.3675 against the greenback, a two-month high.

2) Easing foreign selling: Foreign portfolio investors (FPIs), who have been persistent sellers of Indian equities since late last year, turned buyers in two of the last four sessions, per provisional data.

FIIs/FPIs turned net buyers again on March 20, purchasing shares worth Rs 3,239.14 crore while domestic institutional investors (DII) were net sellers of shares worth Rs 3,136.02 crore, provisional data showed.

During the trading session of March 20, FIIs net bought shares worth Rs 16,328.06 crore and sold shares worth Rs 13,088.92 crore. DIIs purchased shares worth Rs 11,784.06 crore and sold shares worth Rs 14,920.08 crore.

3) Value buying: Attractive valuations in large-cap stocks led to the market rally in last five days, said an analyst.

"Currently, we are witnessing a relief or pull-back rally that could last up to 23,300-23,500 levels, with large-caps trading at attractive valuations," Shrikant Chouhan, head of equity research at Kotak Securities told Reuters.
data.author: MoneyControl

data.title: RVNL shares jump 3% after bagging Rs 554 crore NHAI order in Andhra Pradesh
data.description:
Rail Vikas Nigam Ltd (RVNL)

  • Watchlist
  • Portfolio
  • Message
  • Set Alert
 
 
live
  • bselive
  • nselive
Volume
Todays L/H
More

The shares of Rail Vikas Nigam (RVNL) surged 3 percent on March 21 after the company received letter of acceptance (LoA) from the National Highway Authority of India (NHAI) for a project worth Rs 554 crore.

RVNL said the project involves construction of a six lane, access-controlled connectivity to Visakhapatnam Port Road in Andhra Pradesh, which will connect Sabbavaram bypass on Anakapalli-Anandapuram corridor to Sheelanagar junction of NH51 6C, on a Hybrid Annuity Mode.

The project is to be completed within 730 days as per RVNL's filing on March 21, after the company announced on March 11 that it had emerged as the lowest bidder for the project.

RVNL shares have now extended gains for a fourth consecutive session, rising nearly 13 percent since March 17. The strong momentum in the stock has been helped by a bullish sentiment in the overall market, with Sensex and Nifty clocking strong gains this week. The stock has so far declined over 33 percent in the last six months, and over the past one year, it is down nearly 17 percent.

Also Read: Stock Market updates LIVE

Earlier on March 18, RVNL had announced that the Bombay Stock Exchange (BSE) and the National Stock Exchange (NSE) have each fined the company Rs 5.43 lakh, as half of its board didn’t constitute of independent directors, as seen during the October-December quarter of this financial year. RVNL clarified that it was a government company and the President of India vests with the power to appoint directors on its board. "All Directors in RVNL are appointed by the Government of India through its Administrative Ministry, Ministry of Railways (MoR) and RVNL has no role to play in the appointment of any Director," the company had said in an exchange filing.
data.author: MoneyControl

data.title: UBS Securities believes market bottom is in, sees pockets of opportunities in banking, consumption, industrials
data.description:
The Nifty 50 crossed the 23,300-mark for the first time since early February.

Gautam Chhaochharia and Vishal Goyal of UBS Securities believe the market has found its bottom, citing policy actions from the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) and the government that have successfully reversed the economic direction. While valuations are not overly expensive from both an absolute and relative perspective, the global growth slowdown remains a concern, particularly regarding its indirect impact on India rather than direct tariff-related shocks.

Speaking to CNBC-TV18, the analysts noted that tariffs could pose a short-term shock in early April. However, they anticipate that ongoing bilateral talks may help mitigate their impact. The earnings downgrade cycle for financials, which has persisted for the past year, appears to be stabilizing, though UBS maintains earnings estimates lower than the broader consensus.

Banking poised for recovery

For the banking sector, margins are likely to be affected by repo rate cuts. However, UBS sees valuations in the Banking, Financial Services, and Insurance (BFSI) space as reasonable. The second half of FY26 could potentially mark a recovery in the sector, supported by improving earnings. With a low base in FY25, a double-digit earnings growth projection for FY26 appears reasonable, they added.

Despite these expectations, ground-level data has yet to reflect a full economic recovery, and liquidity remains tight. The advance tax cycle could exert further pressure, and analysts caution that credit growth typically lags behind economic recovery.

Pockets of opportunities

UBS sees select opportunities in PSU utilities, defence, and financial stocks. While they do not expect broad-based thematic moves across the market, they believe individual stocks within sectors will differentiate themselves in this phase of the bull market.

Additionally, the analysts pointed to the communications and wireless (C&W) sector, which, following recent corrections, appears attractive. However, they caution that any disruption by new entrants in the sector could impact incumbent players.

Consumption and Industrials take centerstage

While it is too early to declare a full-fledged bull market return, UBS remains positive on consumption and select industrials. As market sentiment stabilises, sector-specific opportunities and policy-driven tailwinds will play a crucial role in shaping the next phase of growth.
data.author: MoneyControl

data.title: Voltas shares fall over 2.5% as Prabhudas Lilladher downgrades stock to 'accumulate'
data.description:
Voltas shares fall over 2.5% as Prabhudas Lilladher downgrades stock to 'accumulate'

Shares of Voltas Ltd declined over 2% on March 21 after Prabhudas Lilladher downgraded the stock rating to 'accumulate' from 'buy'.

The brokerage firm cut Voltas' rating given the recent sharp rise of the stock.

Prabhudas Lilladher, however, retained the Voltas stock target price at Rs 1,593, which implies 11% upside from the current levels.

At 10:20 am on March 21, Voltas' shares were trading 2.7% lower at Rs 1,430 apiece.

"No major price hikes were announced as the company wants to focus on maintaining its market share. Voltas prioritises volume growth and market share over immediate margin expansion," said Prabhudas Lilladher.

"The company is confident of meeting the increasing demand, but remains cautious about potential demand surges similar to last year. Inventory management and supply chain remain key priorities areas," added the brokerage firm.

Prabhudas Lilladher estimates Voltas' revenue/EBITDA/PAT CAGR of 15%/19.5%/25.3% over FY25-27E. The stock is currently trading at 45x/37x FY26/FY27E.

Meanwhile, Nuvama has reiterated a 'Buy' rating on Voltas, citing strong revenue growth in its Room Air Conditioner segment and the company's focus on absolute profit growth, with a target price of Rs 1,810, implying a 21% upside.
data.author: MoneyControl

data.title: HCC shares rally 13% after JV with Tatas bags Rs 2,500 crore order, second in a week
data.description:
Hindustan Construction shares 13% higher following order win with JV Tata Projects

  • Watchlist
  • Portfolio
  • Message
  • Set Alert
 
 
live
  • bselive
  • nselive
Volume
Todays L/H
More

Shares of Hindustan Construction Company gained about 13 percent in early trading session on March 21, after it secured Rs 2,470 crore order via a joint venture with Tata Projects, soon after a Rs 2,191 crore metro rail project order win earlier this week.

READ more with LIVE updates and news stories

In an exchange filing, the company said,  "Hindustan Construction Company (HCC) and Tata Projects Limited (TPL), in a 50:50 joint venture, have been awarded a Rs 2,470 crore contract by Tata Power Company for the construction of the Bhivpuri Off-Stream Open-Loop Pumped Storage Project (PSP) with a capacity of 1,000 MW (2x333 MW + 2x167 MW), located at Karjat, Maharashtra."

As per the statement, the project entails civil and hydro-mechanical components, including the construction of a coffer dam, intake structures with gates and screens at the existing Thokerwadi reservoir, head race tunnels, penstocks, surge and pressure shafts, a powerhouse, and a tail race tunnel. It involves developing lower intake structures, a new reservoir with a GFRD dam, access roads, drainage systems, adits, and various balance-of-plant foundations and building structures.

This marks the joint venture’s second contract win this week, following a Rs 2,191 crore deal secured on Monday for developing an 8.65 km corridor for the Madhya Pradesh Metro Rail Corporation Limited (MPMRCL), which includes underground tunnels and station construction.

Hindustan Construction Company holds about 55 percent stake in the joint venture with Tata Projects, valued at Rs 1,205 crore.

Disclaimer: The views and investment tips expressed by experts on Moneycontrol.com are their own and not those of the website or its management. Moneycontrol.com advises users to check with certified experts before taking any investment decisions.

data.title: Poonawalla Fincorp Shares Surge 5.37% Amid High Trading Volumes
data.description:
Poonawalla Fincorp Shares Surge 5.37% Amid High Trading Volumes

During today's session, Poonawalla Fincorp shares experienced a significant surge, climbing 5.37% to reach Rs 336.25. The stock's movement was marked by a notable increase in trading volume, attracting attention from investors and analysts alike. This substantial price increase reflects heightened market activity and positive sentiment surrounding the company.

The surge in Poonawalla Fincorp shares is further contextualized by its inclusion in several key indices, including the NIFTY 500, NIFTY Midcap 100, Nifty 200, NIFTY MIDCAP 150, NIFTY MIDSMALLCAP 400, Nifty LargeMidcap 250, and Nifty Total Market. The company’s presence in these indices underscores its significance in the broader market landscape.

Sentiment Analysis


Recent sentiment analysis indicates a shift towards bullish sentiment for Poonawalla Fincorp. Historical ratings show:

This consistent shift towards a bullish outlook may be contributing to the increased investor confidence and the subsequent rise in share price.

data.title: BDL, HAL, other defence stocks surge up to 6% on DAC's nod for Rs 54,000 crore acquisition plans
data.description:
Defence

  • Watchlist
  • Portfolio
  • Message
  • Set Alert
 
 
live
  • bselive
  • nselive
Volume
Todays L/H
More

Shares of leading domestic defence equipment suppliers surged on March 21 after Centre's Defence Acquisition Council (DAC) approved eight capital acquisition proposals worth over Rs 54,000 crore.

The rally in the shares pushed the Nifty India Defence index up over a percent to around 6,245 in early trade, extending gains for the seventh session in a row.

Apollo Micro Systems, which develops the early air warning system - AEW&C - saw its share price surge nearly 4 percent, Bharat Dynamics (BDL) shares surged over 3 percent, riding on the hope that the company may be one of the key beneficiaries in latest approvals to procure Varunastra Torpedoes.

DCX Systems shares were the top gainers on the index, jumping over 6 percent to trade at Rs 254 apiece. ideaForge shares meanwhile surged over 4 percent to trade at Rs 399 apiece. Zen Technologies shares also saw a strong rise, increase over 3 percent to trade at Rs 1,333 apiece.

Hindustan Aeronautics (HAL) and Paras Defence shares were each trading nearly 2 percent higher at Rs 3,897 apiece and Rs 1,010 apiece respectively. Bharat Electronics shares were trading in the green with marginal gains at Rs 298 per share.

The Ministry of Defence announced on March 20 that the DAC has accorded the Acceptance of Necessity (AoNs) to eight proposals. The proposals include the plan to procure 1,350 HP engine to upgrade the present 1,000 HP Engine for Indian Army's T-90 tanks. "This will enhance the battlefield mobility of these tanks especially in high-altitude area by increasing the power to weight ratio," the ministry said.

The proposals also include the procurement of Varunastra Torpedoes (Combat), an indigenously-developed ship-launched anti-submarine torpedo, for the Indian Navy. The further addition of the torpedos which are developed by Naval Science & Technological Laboratory, will "enhance the Navy's capability against adversaries' submarine threats", the ministry said.

For the Indian Air Force, the DAC cleared the proposal for the procurement of Airborne Early Warning & Control (AEW&C) Aircraft Systems. "AEW&C systems are capability enhancers which can change the complete spectrum of warfare and exponentially increase the combat potential of every other weapon system," the ministry further said in its statement.

DAC also approved the norms to make the capital acquisition process faster, more effective and efficient. The government highlighted that 2025 is being celebrated as the 'Year of Reforms’ in the Ministry of Defence.
data.author: MoneyControl
